title: Fix crash when handling empty adb key list
body: This change guards the ADB connection logic against empty or invalid ADB key material. When no keys are parsed or keys are unreadable, the code now gracefully falls back to proceeding without RSA keys (i.e., an empty rsa_keys list) instead of crashing. This preserves a safe failure mode while retaining existing behavior when valid keys are present. Also adds clearer logging around key-loading issues to aid debugging.

step_plan.summary: Guard the ADB connection flow against empty/invalid ADB key material by introducing a safe fallback when keys are missing or unreadable, and optional validation with clearer diagnostics to aid debugging and prevent crashes.

steps.operation: Add defensive handling in _adb_connect to gracefully handle missing/invalid ADB key material by falling back to an empty rsa_keys list
steps.reason: To prevent crashes when key material is missing or unreadable by avoiding constructing a signer with invalid data and by passing an empty rsa_keys list to connect when appropriate; preserves existing behavior when valid keys are present.
steps.side_effects: Adds a safer fallback path for empty/invalid keys; may require updates to tests to cover empty-key scenarios; may affect user prompts/authorization behavior if devices require keys for ADB authorization; logs will indicate the fallback path and key-loading issues.

steps.operation: Add validation and logging in _adb_check_keys to verify loaded keys are non-empty and readable
steps.reason: To provide early diagnostics and a clear decision point for fallback behavior by verifying key material before it is used, complementing the guard in _adb_connect.
steps.side_effects: Introduces logs for unreadable/empty keys; may require tests to simulate invalid key material; could influence downstream behavior if callers rely on _adb_check_keys returning specific signals about key validity.
